title: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Claremont Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Claremont Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of any local ordinances/bylaws (under RSA 31:39) and other policies regulating conflicts of interest, financial disclosures, reporting requirements and incompatibility standards for municipial officers and employees.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Property managers receiving Dec. 8 letter

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Property managers receiving Dec. 8 letter
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of the contacts at 13 property management software providers—including name, title, and company—that were sent a letter/email on December 8 regarding advertising total leasing prices. 
The letter was sent from Christopher Mufarrige, Director of the FTC’s Bureau of Consumer Protection.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tracie McMillan

communications.subject: Acknowledgment and Inform of Backlog
communications.communication:
Good morning,



Thank you for your reply. This correspondence confirms the receipt of your FOIA/PA request, seeking " list of 13 Property management Software Providers received Warning Letters (Press Release Dec. 9. 2025)." (simplified)



Due to the significant backlog caused by the federal shutdown, the FTC is processing requests as quickly as possible but has not yet begun to process yours. As soon as we are able, an individual FTC employee will reach out to let you know that they are starting work on your request and will provide their contact information.



In the meantime, if you have questions or concerns, please reach out to us at foia@ftc.gov<mailto:foia@ftc.gov>. You may also check the status of your FOIA request by visiting http://www.ftc.gov/about-ftc/foia/foia-reading-rooms/track-your-foia-request. This report is updated at the end of each month. If you should have any questions regarding your request, please contact us at foia@ftc.gov<mailto:foia@ftc.gov>. In any call or future correspondence concerning the request, please refer to the following case number FOIA-2026-00153.
Best,

FOIA Team

title: Request for Body-worn Camera Footage: Niani Finlayson (12/4/2023)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for Body-worn Camera Footage: Niani Finlayson (12/4/2023)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All unedited body-worn camera / bodycam recordings related to the following law enforcement critical incident: 

Incident Date: December 4, 2023
Location: 2105 E Ave J 8, Lancaster, CA 
Victim Name: Niani Finlayson

I am specifically requesting the unedited body-worn camera recordings captured by all officers present for the full duration of the incident, including audio recordings associated with the footage. 

If any portion of the requested footage must be redacted, I ask that you clearly indicate the portions that have been redacted and provide an explanation of the legal basis for the redactions. If the requested footage is withheld in its entirety, please provide a detailed explanation citing relevant public records exemption(s).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kelly Stevenson

communications.subject: Public Records Request LASD-26-02192041 Update TRACK:00059583
communications.communication:
Attention: Kelly Stevenson

Dear Ms./Mr. Stevenson;

This message is regarding your request for records under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) received by the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department on January 10, 2025*. Your Request has been assigned a reference number of: LASD-26-02192041 (This number may be different from the original Request Number we assigned (25-15403SE) as the Department began using a new system to manage PRA requests starting January 5, 2026. You may refer to either number when contacting us.). In your request you are seeking the following:

DECEMBER 31, 2024 TO WHOM IT MAY CONCERN: PURSUANT TO THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT, I HEREBY REQUEST THE FOLLOWING RECORDS: ALL UNEDITED BODY-WORN CAMERA / BODYCAM RECORDINGS RELATED TO THE FOLLOWING LAW ENFORCEMENT CRITICAL INCIDENT: INCIDENT DATE: DECEMBER 4, 2023 LOCATION: 2105 E AVEJ 8, LANCASTER, CA VICTIM NAME: NIANI FINLAYSON I AM SPECIFICALLY REQUESTING THE UNEDITED BODY-WORN CAMERA RECORDINGS CAPTURED BY ALL OFFICERS PRESENT FOR THE FULL DURATION OF THE INCIDENT, INCLUDING AUDIO RECORDINGS ASSOCIATED WITH THE FOOTAGE. IF ANY PORTION OF THE REQUESTED FOOTAGE MUST BE REDACTED, I ASK THAT YOU CLEARLY INDICATE THE PORTIONS THAT HAVE BEEN REDACTED AND PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION OF THE LEGAL BASIS FOR THE REDACTIONS. IF THE REQUESTED FOOTAGE IS WITHHELD IN ITS ENTIRETY, PLEASE PROVIDE A DETAILED EXPLANATION CITING RELEVANT PUBLIC RECORDS EXEMPTION(S). THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE GENERAL PUBLIC, AND THIS REQUEST IS NOT BEING MADE FOR COMMERCIAL PURPOSES. IN THE EVENT THAT THERE ARE FEES, I WOULD BE GRATEFUL IF YOU WOULD INFORM ME OF THE TOTAL CHARGES IN ADVANCE OF FULFILLING MY REQUEST. I WOULD PREFER THE REQUEST FILLED ELECTRONICALLY, BY E-MAIL ATTACHMENT IF AVAILABLE OR CD-ROM IF NOT. THANK YOU IN ADVANCE FOR YOUR ANTICIPATED COOPERATION IN THIS MATTER. I LOOK FORWARD TO RECEIVING YOUR RESPONSE TO THIS REQUEST WITHIN 10 CALENDAR DAYS, AS THE STATUTE REQUIRES. SINCERELY, IF PROMPTED FOR A PASSCODE, PLEASE ENTER: XAJFYCEM FILED VIA MUCKROCK.COM E-MAIL (PREFERRED): 178784-24 795087@REQUESTS.MUCKROCK.COM PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS FOR MAILED RESPONSES, PLEASE ADDRESS (SEE NOTE): MUCKROCK NEWS DEPT MR 178784 263 HUNTINGTON AVE BOSTON, MA 02115 PLEASE NOTE: THIS REQUEST IS NOT FILED BY A MUCKROCK STAFF MEMBER, BUT IS BEING SENT THROUGH MUCKROCK BY THE ABOVE IN ORDER TO BETTER TRACK, SHARE, AND MANAGE PUBLIC RECORDS REQUESTS. ALSO NOTE THAT IMPROPERLY ADDRESSED (I.E., WITH THE REQUESTER'S NAME RATHER THAN "MUCKROCK NEWS" AND THE DEPARTMENT NUMBER) REQUESTS MIGHT BE RETURNED AS UNDELIVERABLE. KELLY STEVENSON VIEW REQUEST HISTORY, UPLOAD RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, AND REPORT PROBLEMS HERE: HTTPS://WWW .MUCKROCK.COM/RESPOND/1902608/

At the time we received your request we anticipated being able to provide a response by February 03, 2025, however, at this time we do not yet have a response available. The status of your request is R: Received.

We will continue to provide you with periodic updates as necessary.

Additionally, the Department began using a new system to manage public records requests and, because your original request was made prior to the above date it was moved over to our new system. Should you notice any problems with your request (e.g. spelling of your name, salutation, request details etc. please reply to this email to let us know so we can make any needed corrections.

If you have any questions, please contact the Risk Management Bureau PRA Unit SB-1421 Team at 323-890-5050. You may also reply to this email to either provide additional information or withdraw your request.

Note: When responding to this email do not remove the tracking number in the subject line or we may not receive your message.

Sincerely,

ROBERT G. LUNA, SHERIFF

[Captain Signature]

Tri T. Hoang, Captain

Risk Management Bureau

* This date may differ from the date of your request as requests received after hours, on weekends or holidays are entered the next business day.

MESSAGE: UPDTSCHD135-Fallback

title: Release of Mugshots for Damien Echols

communications.subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Release of Mugshots for Damien Echols
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

We are seeking all mugshots taken of Damien Echols during his time of imprisonment from 1993 - 2011.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Erikka Yancy

communications.subject: RE: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Release of Mugshots for Damien Echols
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Pursuant to A.C.A. § 12-27-113(e)(2)(A)(i), disclosure or inspection of information contained in inmate records is prohibited unless authorized by Administrative Rule or by court order.
 Administrative Rules 804(IV) allows the following information from an inmate record to be released:
1. Offender's name, aliases, ADC number, photograph, physical description, date of birth, age, race, and gender.
2. Date of incarceration.
3. Facility(ies) of confinement, work assignments, and program participation (unless it involves drug and alcohol program participation).
4. The following information regarding current incarceration: offense (s), sentence (s), sentence date (s), county (ies) of conviction, case number (s), total time to serve, parole/transfer eligibility date, movements and behavior.
5. The following information regarding prior incarceration(s): offense(s), sentence(s), sentence date(s), county(ies) of conviction, case number(s), movements, behavior and parole/clemency history, including date(s) of release and date(s) of return to ADC.
6. Detainers.
I have attached the disclosable information responsive to your request.
Kind regards,

Miles S. Morgan


Miles Morgan
ATTORNEY III
Shared Services | Department of Corrections
e: Miles.S.Morgan@doc.arkansas.gov
t: 501-682-9540

title: Highest-value RFPs (Troy Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Highest-value RFPs (Troy Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

Copies of all bids submitted in response to the five highest-value RFPs issued by this agency, with proposal openings between January 1, 2015 and the date this request is fulfilled. Please include any associated records identifying the RFP number, title, and awarded vendor, as well as requests for clarification and communications with the losing bidders.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kelly Kauffman

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. title: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA - Ventura County - Flock Safety Documents (Ventura Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Organization Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches conducted within the agency.

- Network Audit in Flock. 
All data logged from July 1, 2023 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, this report is available within the Insights tab and reflects searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock system.

- Hardware.
The current number and installed locations of any Flock Safety, LPR,  AVIS, or machine learning-assisted camera systems (provide locations as coordinates or address with nearest cross-street), and any documents related to planned future installations of the same.

- Documents.
>  Invoices, contracts, purchase orders, and bid documents related to the acquisition or renewal of Flock Safety products or services
>  Agreements with homeowner associations, private businesses, or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
>  Training materials, policies, and SOPs related to Flock Safety platform use
>  Grant or funding records related to the Flock system
>  Data retention policies for data collected by Flock Safety systems
>  Any complaints or grievances received from the public regarding ALPR camera placement or data use


I am not requesting personally identifiable information or license plate data. If any responsive record contains such information, please redact only that portion and produce the remainder. If any records are withheld, please provide a written index identifying each withheld record and the statutory exemption claimed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jordan

title: Providence removal of Iryna Zarutska mural

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Providence removal of Iryna Zarutska mural
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any records or other COMMUNICATIONS to or from the Office of the Mayor (including Mayor Brett Smiley) since March 1, 2026, concerning the Iryna Zarutska mural.

2. Any COMMUNICATIONS to or from the Office of the Mayor (including Mayor Brett Smiley) since March 1, 2026, otherwise mentioning any of the following terms: Zarutska, Gaudreau, "Dark Lady".

3. Any COMMUNICATIONS between the City of Providence and/or the Office of the Mayor and the owners or operators of the Dark Lady after March 1, 2026.

"COMMUNICATIONS" means and includes emails, text messages, direct messages sent via social media (but not including comments or messages posted for public view), letters, memoranda, calendar entries, chats, or any other form of one-to-one or one-to-many communication.

Please note that this request seeks public records wherever they may be found, including on devices or networks owned or operated by private parties. For example, if an employee uses a personal cell phone or social media account to conduct public business, records created, maintained, or held on the phone or account are public records subject to this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

communications.subject: Your City of Providence public records request #26-666 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Providence ************************************************************************ Record request #26-666 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Requester: The Public Records Unit has conducted an initial review of the responsive documents and has determined that it will require a significant amount of search, retrieval, review, and/or redaction to respond to your request. More specifically, it is estimated that it will take11.5 hours. Under the Access to Public Records Act, the City may charge $15 per hour for search, retrieval, review, and redaction, with the first hour free. See R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-4(b). The City has already expended a minimum of 1 hour in assessing your request and review potentially responsive documents. Accordingly, if you wish to proceed, you will be charged $172. Please note that this is just an estimate and you will be charged based upon actual time and costs. Please further note that nothing in this email represents a determination that responsive records will be found or that, if found, responsive records are public records. Kindly note that the time to respond to your request is tolled pending receipt of your authorization and prepayment in the amount set forth in the estimate. Checks made payable to the City of Providence may be sent to: Public Records Unit, 444 Westminster Street, Suite 220, Providence, RI 02903. In the Memo or "For" section, please write the request number (i.e. Request 26-666). Accordingly, this request is administratively closed pending prepayment. As soon as the City receives payment, it will reopen this request. Thank you.   ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Providence.

communications.subject: Your City of Providence public records request #26-666 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Providence ************************************************************************ Record request #26-666 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Requester: The City has released records responsive to your Request. Please note that these have been redacted to remove information “the disclosure of which would constitute [an] unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.” RIGL § 38-2-2(4)(A)(I)(b). Documents have also been redacted to protect copyright. See id. at § 38-2-2(4)(S). Additionally, documents, including emails and social media direct messages, on the following bases: they include “correspondence of or to elected officials with or relating to those they represent and correspondence of or to elected officials in their official capacities” and/or because they relate to a client/attorney relationship. See id. at § 38-2-2(4)(A)(I)(a), (M). No portion of the withheld documents contains reasonably segregable information that is releasable. R.I.G.L. § 38-2-3(b). Furthermore, the City has expended the full 11.5 hours in search and review of the responsive documents. However, the City’s initial search was limited to the period of March 1, 2026 through April 2, 2026. Review of additional materials is estimated to take an additional 5 hours. Under the Access to Public Records Act, the City may charge $15 per hour for search, retrieval, review, and redaction. See R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-4(b). Accordingly, if you wish to proceed and have the City review materials for the time period of April 2, 2026 through May 21, 2026, you will be charged an additional $75. Checks made payable to the City of Providence may be sent to: Public Records Unit, 444 Westminster Street, Suite 220, Providence, RI 02903. In the Memo or "For" section, please write the request number (i.e., Request 26-666). Please note that this is just an estimate and you will be charged based upon actual time and costs. Please further note that nothing in this email represents a determination that responsive records will be found or that, if found, responsive records are public records. Kindly note that the time to respond to your request is tolled pending receipt of your authorization and prepayment in the amount set forth in the estimate.  In accordance with R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-8, you may wish to appeal this decision to the Chief Administrative Officer. In this instance, Emily Crowell, Chief of Staff, fulfills that role. You may also wish to file a complaint with the Department of the Attorney General or the Rhode Island Superior Court. This request is now closed, if you notify us that you would like additional records and once we have received payment as explained above, we will reopen this request. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Providence.

title: James Dobson

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: James Dobson
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation, inquiry of, or correspondence with James Clayton Dobson, Jr. (April 21, 1936 - August 21, 2025). Proof of death is not required as his death was widely reported: https://www.nytimes.com/2025/08/21/us/politics/james-dobson-dead.html Please conduct a cross reference search with the following organizations: 

- CitizenLink
- Family Policy Alliance
- Family Policy Council
- Family Research Council
- Focus on the Family
- Focus on the Family Action
- Focus on the Family Canada
- Focus on the Family Singapore
- National Day of Prayer Task Force

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals,

records are located and processed for release. The requested documents will be
made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for
commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if
you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.
I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if
available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated
cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this
request within 20 business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Paul
Galante Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2132939/

Supporting documentation

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

title: Dental amalgams and Minamata Convention comms

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Dental amalgams and Minamata Convention comms
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All communications, documents, and reports possessed by the Indian Health Service and IHS Division of Oral Health related to the phase down of dental amalgam as well as the Minamata Convention on Mercury - both referenced here on the IHS' Dental Portal: https://www.ihs.gov/DOH/index.cfm?fuseaction=home.amalgam

This should include materials prepared for submission to the Secretariat of the Minamata Convention on Mercury, and documents related to compliance measures with the Convention.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tristan Ahtone

title: Public Records Request — California Generative AI Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — California Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Coordinator
California Department of Technology
P.O. Box 1810
Rancho Cordova, CA 95741

Dear Public Records Coordinator,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to California's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including the Poppy GenAI platform developed by the California Department of Technology.

We are requesting the following records under Cal. Gov. Code § 6253(c):

1. All interaction logs from Poppy, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from September 25, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of Poppy or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for Poppy showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Poppy is an enterprise generative AI platform built by CDT for California state employees, utilizing 11 different AI models and grounded on public state data. As of early 2026, Poppy has more than 2,600 users across 66 departments, with over 20 state departments involved in its development and testing (see California Department of Technology, "Poppy: California's Digital Assistant," https://www.genai.ca.gov/poppy/; see also StateScoop, "Only California state employees can use Poppy, a new AI assistant designed for security," https://statescoop.com/california-poppy-state-government-ai-assistant/; GovTech, "California Looks to Drive Efficiency With AI Assistant," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/california-looks-to-drive-efficiency-with-ai-assistant). Governor Newsom has also launched initiatives to accelerate responsible AI in state government (see Governor of California, Dec. 16, 2025, https://www.gov.ca.gov/2025/12/16/governor-newsom-launches-new-initiatives-to-partner-with-tech-policy-experts-and-accelerate-responsible-ai-in-state-government/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The California Public Records Act defines "public records" to include any writing containing information relating to the conduct of the public's business, regardless of physical form or characteristics, encompassing electronic communications and digital files. Cal. Gov. Code § 7920.530. Governor Newsom's Executive Order N-12-23 (September 2023) directed state agencies to develop a generative AI framework, and the CalGovOps GenAI Policy Framework (2024) requires agencies to maintain inventories of AI/GenAI tools and document use cases. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Laconia Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Laconia Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by the chair of the select board related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.communication:
Dear Rose  Terse: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Jefferson County. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 3/30/2026 and given the reference number C003075-033026 for tracking purposes. 
 
Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2252946/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant County department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.   Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Jefferson County 
 If you do not see an acknowledgement/Confirmation email from jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net please check your junk mail. You may need to add jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net to your safe senders list.  Communications with Jefferson County in regards to your public records request will come from this email address.

title: Boston Police Department Internal Affairs Compliment and Complaint

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Boston Police Department Internal Affairs Compliment and Complaint
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Boston Police Department Internal Affairs Compliment and Complaints filed for the year 2018. Specifically, we request an extract of the records to include the following columns:
- Incident number
- Officer name
- Officer City of Boston ID (6-digits)
- Report narrative

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rachel Young

Reference No: B000834-032124 Dear Rachel Young: The Boston Police Department (BPD) has received your request for public records.  This response applies only to records that exist and are in the custody of the BPD. See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, p. 32, n.115.  It is expected that a custodian of records must use her superior knowledge of her records with respect to responses to public records requests.  950 CMR 32.04(5).  Specifically, you stated: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Boston Police Department Internal Affairs Compliment and Complaints filed for the year 2018. Specifically, we request an extract of the records to include the following columns: 
- Incident number 
- Officer name 
- Officer City of Boston ID (6-digits) 
- Report narrative 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rachel Young 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fboston-police-department-174%252Fboston-police-department-internal-affairs-compliment-and-complaint-160889%252F%253Femail%253Dshawn.williams%252540boston.gov&url_auth_token=AAAj3ZlQ8Z6owDLMtztfSTxotfQ%3A1rnSxI%3AWbV2uX0O4sjWeE7cfH11UmGENRWp3H3Lfg5coSz4mdo A response to a public records request must be provided within ten (10) business days from the business day a written request was received.  G. L. c. 66, § 10 (a); 950 CMR 32.06(2)(b).  The public records law permits a response time of up to twenty-five (25) business days from the business day a written request is received, so long as a detailed explanation is provided.  G. L. c. 66, § 10(b)(vi); 950 CMR 32.06(2)(i). Due to the nature of your request additional time is needed.  As such, BPD will need up to, but no more than, fifteen (15) additional business days to review and process your request.

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: B000834-032124
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/21/2026 Public Record Request of 3/21/2024 8:42:57 PM, Reference No:B000834-032124 Dear Rachel Young: The Boston Police Department (Department) received your request for public records.  This letter shall act as the response to your request for public records. Please note this response will apply only to the records that exist and are in the custody of the Department. See A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, p. 32, n.115.  It is expected that a custodian of records must user her superior knowledge of her records with respect to responses to public records requests.  See 950 C.M.R. 32.04(5). Specifically, you requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Boston Police Department Internal Affairs Compliment and Complaints filed for the year 2018. Specifically, we request an extract of the records to include the following columns: 
- Incident number 
- Officer name 
- Officer City of Boston ID (6-digits) 
- Report narrative 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rachel Young 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fboston-police-department-174%252Fboston-police-department-internal-affairs-compliment-and-complaint-160889%252F%253Femail%253Dshawn.williams%252540boston.gov&url_auth_token=AAAj3ZlQ8Z6owDLMtztfSTxotfQ%3A1rnSxI%3AWbV2uX0O4sjWeE7cfH11UmGENRWp3H3Lfg5coSz4mdo We have reviewed our files and have located records that correspond to your request. These records have been released to the . title: FOIA Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
Pursuant to PA 523 of 2018, ELPD may reject any FOIA request that does not include the requesting person's complete name, address, and contact information. Additionally, an address must be written in compliance with United States Postal Service addressing standards. Contact information must include a valid telephone number or electronic mail address. MCL 15.233 et seq.

Laurel Briggs

East Lansing, MI 48823

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Luce County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Luce County)
communications.communication:
Dear Luce County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Luce County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: Public Records Request: Fusus logs (Kernersville Police Department)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Fusus logs (Kernersville Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Audit logs/audit trails documenting each time a department account has accessed live or recorded video footage through the Fusus platforms from 1/1/2024 until the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: Public Records Request
communications.communication:
To whom it may concern,



Thank you for submitting your public records request on May 13, 2026.



Pursuant to North Carolina law and in accordance with North Carolina General Statute Chapter 132 regarding public records, certain audit logs maintained by law enforcement agencies may be partially exempt from disclosure. Under N.C.G.S. § 132-1.4, records related to criminal investigations or criminal intelligence activities may be withheld or redacted when disclosure could reveal investigative methods, specific searches, tactical procedures, or information connected to an active investigation. Additionally, agencies may redact employee identifiers, network security information, and other sensitive technical details to preserve the integrity and security of internal systems.



Accordingly, I have provided the responsive information available for release. The records provided cover the date range of 01/01/2024 through 05/19/2026. During this timeframe, the department accessed live or recorded video footage through the FUSUS platform on 1,498 occasions (see attached records).



Regards,


LIEUTENANT F.L. SANCHEZ (K99)
KERNERSVILLE POLICE DEPARTMENT
Criminal Investigative Division/Professional Standards
134 E. Mountain Street
Kernersville, NC  27284
fsanchez@toknc.com

[cid:image001.png@01DCE8F8.2D181A40]



________________________________
This email message, and any attachment(s) hereto, as well as any email message(s) that may be sent in response to it, may be considered Public Record, and as such are subject to requests for review. If you are not the intended recipient, please destroy this message and inform the sender immediately. The information contained in this email may be confidential and, in any event, is intended only for the use of the entity or individual to whom it is addressed. Thank you.

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Quincy Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

title: Nicodemo Scarfo

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Nicodemo Scarfo
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

ALL documents mentioning Nicodemo Domenic "Little Nicky" Scarfo (March 8, 1929 – January 13, 2017). This will be the THIRD time I've requested it. DO NOT LIMIT THE RESPONSE TO PREPROCESSED RECORDS. DOING SO WILL BE TAKEN AS AN OUTRIGHT ADMISSION OF DELIBERATE BAD FAITH.

Please and thank you.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires, though you seem quite happy to knowingly break this law by huge, unrepentant margins.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

P.S. 

ACAB

title: 2026 Brady List (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Walla Walla County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Your Walla Walla County public records request #26-9 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
Walla Walla County ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-9 has been submitted successfully. This letter serves as Walla Walla County’s initial five-day letter, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, to your public records request. After your request is reviewed for content and we determine the department or office in which your request refers to, the County will provide an estimate to provide an initial installment of responsive records. After locating responsive records, the County will determine whether any of the responsive documents are subject to applicable exemptions under the Public Records Act, and to notify any third parties affected by the request. If third party notification is required, twenty (20) business days will be allowed for a response by the third party to those records which specifically pertain to them. As records are reviewed, they may be released on an installment basis. Additionally, the Washington Public Records Act (RCW 42.56.120) and Walla Walla County Resolution 23-201 now allow the County to charge for provision of electronic records. Refer to the hyperlinked documents for the fee schedule for future records installments. The due date for your request may be later than usual because the office is closed on: * January 19: Martin Luther King, Jr. Day * February 16: Presidents' Day ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Walla Walla County.

Change your email settings | Visit our help center

title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Kent Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Kent Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
	l. 50295D  — SWAT cutaway van (2008)
	m. 54117D  — CSI van (2011)
	n. 24208D  — Weapons vehicle (1987)
	
	Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern):
	o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP"
	p. All plates with the prefix "XMT"
	
	Other:
	a. BCH-478
	b. 826-ZVA

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

but vehicles are believed to remain in service): 
c. 50816D — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010) 
d. 50887D — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031) 
e. 54180D — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031) 
f. 43500D — Bomb squad trailer (2005) 
g. 26073D — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997) 
h. 41461D — Communications van (2005)

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238467/ Your

Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ## 

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production. 

## FEE REQUEST ## 

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced. 

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request: 

Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles 
(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered 
but vehicles are believed to remain in service): 
c. 50816D — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010) 
d. 50887D — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031) 
e. 54180D — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031) 
f. 43500D — Bomb squad trailer (2005) 
g. 26073D — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997) 
h. 41461D — Communications van (2005) 
i. 44372D — Command vehicle (2005) 
j. 54148D — Riot fencing trailer (2011) 
k. 54362D — CSI van with generator (2012) 
l. 50295D — SWAT cutaway van (2008) 
m. 54117D — CSI van (2011) 
n. 24208D — Weapons vehicle (1987) 

Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern): 
o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP" 
p. All plates with the prefix "XMT" 

Other: 
a. BCH-478 
b. 826-ZVA 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238467/ The City advised you it anticipated responding to your request within 5 business days. The City now needs  to extend this estimate; and currently estimates it will be able to provide responsive records, either on an installment basis or in total by 04/02/2026. 
This additional time is necessary due to current staffing levels, current volume of public record requests received; locating and assembling the requested records; if necessary notifying any third parties who may be affected by the request; and to determine whether any of the information contained within the requested records is exempt from disclosure, in whole or in part. If you have any questions, please contact the City Clerk’s Office at 253-856-5725. Thank you. Sincerely, Tiffany Alcorn Tiffany Alcorn CPRO - Lead Public Disclosure Administrator City of Kent Administration 
Arthur “Pat” Fitzpatrick, Chief Administrative Officer

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt. 

Please immediately: 

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval. 

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation.
